title: NOC 14112 – Desktop Publishing Operators and Related Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Desktop Publishing Operators and related professionals play a vital role in the publishing and printing industries by preparing layouts, typesetting materials, and producing print-ready digital content across public and private sectors in Canada. Classified under NOC 14112, this occupation has seen growing post-pandemic demand and is expected to remain in steady need—creating realistic immigration opportunities through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Growth in digital-to-print workflowsOngoing demand for high-quality publishing materialsEntry-level TEER occupation with technical specializationEmployer-supported recruitmentExpansion of in-house printing operations
classification.major_group: 141 – Office, court, and data support occupations
classification.minor_group: 1411 – Survey, statistical, and data entry occupations

duties: Operate desktop publishing software and equipment to design, lay out, and produce camera-ready copy
Mark copy with computer codes and typographic instructions before typesetting
Input copy, codes, commands, and corrections into typesetting systems
Operate media-conversion equipment to process and format transmitted content
Operate and maintain output devices and photosensitive materials
employment_requirements: Completion of a college program in graphic arts, typography, computer typesetting, or desktop publishing (usually required)
Technical familiarity with publishing and layout software
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 2,300 new positions for Desktop Publishing Operators and related occupations, while only about 1,300 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Desktop Publishing Operators and related professionals are in demand across publishing, printing, and in-house corporate production departments nationwide. Hiring has expanded steadily since the pandemic and is expected to continue over the coming years.
Typesetting and graphic production firmsCommercial printing companiesPublishing housesGovernment print departmentsCorporate in-house design and printing units
Annual salaries typically range between $28,900 and $68,300 CAD, depending on province, experience, and technical expertise.
